site.name: Kiran Gorapalli
site.description: Portfolio site for Kiran Gorapalli - product manager focused on the human side of building products.

teardown.description: Product analyses of real companies. Each teardown follows a structured format: Discovery (persona + journey map), Keep/Kill/Build (what to preserve, cut, and create), The Redesign (wireframes showing proposed changes), and The Business Case (KPIs and projected impact).

teardown-hub.description: Company-level index pages that group related teardowns. Each hub lists the individual product teardowns for a single company.

prototype.description: Working software demonstrating product thinking. Each prototype has an overview page explaining the problem, architecture, and design decisions, plus a live interactive version.

blog.description: Long-form articles on product management, AI adoption, and building products. Written from practitioner experience, not theory.

hub-page.description: Index pages that group content by type - MadLab for prototypes, Studio for creative work, How I'd've Built It for teardowns.

content.title: GEICO Mobile App Teardown

persona.name: Alex T.
persona.context: Switched from State Farm, commutes daily, doesn't think about insurance unless something goes wrong

connections.description: The prototype was built to demonstrate the redesign proposed in this teardown
Listed on the GEICO company hub page

content.summary: Analysis of GEICO's mobile app AI assistant. The assistant uses keyword matching instead of NLP, can't handle multi-step requests, and drops context on handoff to human agents. Proposed redesign uses a LangGraph-powered conversational engine with smart escalation.

content.title: Instagram Teardown

persona.name: Maya R.
persona.context: Instagram user navigating content discovery and following-first experience

connections.description: Listed on the Meta company hub page

content.summary: Analysis of Instagram's content discovery experience. Examines how the algorithmic feed has shifted away from following-first content, proposes a redesign that puts followed accounts back in focus with a session digest feature.

content.title: GEICO Teardowns
content.summary: Company hub page for GEICO product teardowns. Currently features the mobile app teardown with more planned.

content.title: Meta Teardowns
content.summary: Company hub page for Meta product teardowns. Currently features the Instagram teardown with WhatsApp, Facebook, and Threads planned.

content.title: Spotify Teardowns
content.summary: Company hub page for Spotify product teardowns. Currently features the Playlist Discovery teardown with Wrapped and Podcasts planned.

content.title: Amazon Mobile App Teardown
content.focusArea: Mobile search and product discovery for time-pressed users

persona.name: Maria Santos
persona.context: 34, procurement manager, Austin, working mom navigating Amazon mobile search

connections.description: Listed on the Amazon company hub page

content.summary: Analysis of Amazon's mobile app search experience. Examines how search chaos, filter overload, and lack of session persistence kill task completion for time-pressed users. Proposes intent-based search modes, visual session history, and contextual smart filters.

content.title: Amazon Teardowns
content.summary: Company hub page for Amazon product teardowns. Currently features the Mobile App teardown with Prime Video and Alexa planned.

content.title: Insurance AI Assistant

connections.description: Built to demonstrate the redesign proposed in the GEICO mobile app teardown

content.summary: Working chatbot prototype that handles multi-step insurance claims. Uses LangGraph for stateful conversation management so the bot remembers context across turns. Includes real-time state visualisation showing the graph traversal.

content.title: Jurassic Bites

connections.description: Companion prototype showing intent-based NLP (Dialogflow) vs stateful AI (LangGraph) for chatbot design

content.summary: Restaurant reservation chatbot built on Google Dialogflow ES with 17 intents, 4 custom entities, and context-managed booking flows. A case study in what intent-based NLP does well (entity extraction, structured Q&A) and where it falls short (multi-turn memory, dynamic responses).

content.title: Onboardly

connections.description: Companion prototype — Jurassic Bites uses pre-written responses, Onboardly generates answers from documents
Companion prototype — Insurance AI uses stateful conversation graphs, Onboardly uses retrieval-augmented generation

content.summary: RAG-powered HR onboarding assistant that answers employee questions by searching a corpus of 8 policy documents. Uses TF-IDF retrieval with cosine similarity to find relevant chunks, then sends them to Claude for grounded generation. Demonstrates how RAG keeps AI honest — it only says what it can back up.

content.title: Demystifying ACH Payments
sections: Same-Day ACH and the Speed Race
Why This Matters for Product Managers
content.summary: A payments PM's guide to the ACH network that quietly moves $80 trillion a year. Explains how ACH works from the ground up - the batch processing model, settlement windows, same-day ACH, and why understanding these rails matters when building payment products. Includes a personal anecdote from Wells Fargo about the gap between instant UI and T+2 settlement.

content.title: The Bank That Got 213,000 Employees to Actually Use AI
sections: The Password Reset That Changed Everything
The Resistance Problem Nobody Wants to Admit
Start With the Problem, Not the Technology
What Happens When You Cut Before You Convert
content.summary: Case study of Bank of America's AI adoption strategy. BofA got 90% of 213,000 employees using AI not through mandates but by solving real problems like password resets. The article argues that successful AI adoption starts with unglamorous problems, not flashy demos.

content.title: Spotify Playlist Discovery Teardown
content.focusArea: Playlist discovery and music recommendation for long-term users

persona.name: Alex M.
persona.context: Long-term Spotify user trying to evolve musical taste beyond established patterns

connections.description: Listed on the Spotify company hub page

content.summary: Analysis of Spotify's playlist discovery experience for long-term users. Examines how the recommendation algorithm reinforces existing listening patterns instead of helping users evolve their taste. Proposes a redesign with discovery intent settings and context-aware listening to give users more control over music exploration.

hubPages.title: MadLab

hubPages.title: Studio
hubPages.description: Creative work hub - passion projects, not deliverables

relationships.teardown-to-prototype: A teardown identifies problems in a real product; a prototype demonstrates a proposed solution. The teardown provides the 'why', the prototype provides the 'how'.
relationships.prototype-to-skills: Each prototype demonstrates specific product and technical skills through working software.
relationships.blog-to-theme: Blog posts explore themes that appear across teardowns and prototypes, providing deeper analysis and opinion.
relationships.teardown-hub-to-teardown: Company hub pages group individual teardowns for the same company.
relationships.studio-to-none: Studio work is personal and creative. It connects to Kiran as a person, not directly to his professional work.
